_comment: Multi-doc eval set bound to the live 'very big' collection (id 1c485387-3fd0-4608-a677-64d724323f90). 8 docs: Google 10-K FY2021/2022/2023 (goog-2021/2022/20231231.pdf), Microsoft 10-K FY2021/2022/2023 (msft-10k_20210630.htm.pdf, msft-10k_20220630.htm.pdf, 0000950170-23-035122.pdf), Amazon 10-K FY2022/2023 (amzn-20221231.pdf, amzn-20231231.pdf). Now that 8 docs >= ROUTING_MAX_FANOUT, gold_doc_filenames is a SUBSET, so routing can actually miss — this is a real ranking test. ground_truth is descriptive, not exact figures.
question: What are Microsoft's three reportable operating segments in its fiscal 2023 annual report?
ground_truth: Productivity and Business Processes; Intelligent Cloud; and More Personal Computing. Single-doc needle: routing must select the Microsoft FY2023 10-K specifically out of 8 docs.
_note: Needle test — the answer lives in ONE of 8 docs. Tests routing precision, not just recall.

question: How did Google's total revenue change across fiscal years 2021, 2022, and 2023?
ground_truth: Google/Alphabet total revenue grew year over year across 2021->2022->2023 (roughly $257B, $283B, $307B). A trend question that requires all three Google 10-Ks and no others.
_note: Same-company multi-year trend — routing must pick the 3 Google docs and exclude Microsoft/Amazon.

question: How did Microsoft describe the growth of its Intelligent Cloud / Azure business from fiscal 2021 through fiscal 2023?
ground_truth: Microsoft's Intelligent Cloud segment and Azure grew strongly each year across FY2021-FY2023, driven by Azure consumption. Requires all three Microsoft 10-Ks.
_note: Microsoft multi-year trend — routing must pick the 3 Microsoft docs.

question: Compare the fiscal 2023 cloud businesses of Google, Microsoft, and Amazon: how does each position its cloud segment?
ground_truth: Google Cloud (GCP + Workspace), Microsoft Azure within Intelligent Cloud, and Amazon AWS — each described as a strategic growth segment in their FY2023 filings. Requires the three FY2023 10-Ks, one per company.
_note: Cross-company same-year comparison — routing must pick one FY2023 doc from each of three companies.

question: How did Amazon's net sales and operating income change from fiscal 2022 to fiscal 2023?
ground_truth: Amazon net sales grew from FY2022 to FY2023 and operating income improved substantially in FY2023 versus FY2022. Requires the two Amazon 10-Ks only.
_note: Amazon year-over-year — routing must pick the 2 Amazon docs and exclude the other 6.

question: Across all three companies' most recent filings, what common themes appear in how they describe investment in artificial intelligence?
ground_truth: All three emphasize AI as central to strategy and future investment: Google (Search/Cloud/DeepMind/Gemini), Microsoft (Azure AI, Copilot, OpenAI partnership), Amazon (AWS AI/ML services, generative AI). A global question spanning the three FY2023 filings.
_note: Global/thematic across the three latest filings.
